sectiondef.header: K-Factor Values for Line Rate Ranges

briefdescription.para: CLKOUT0 Reg1: even divide, no edge.
CLKOUT0 Reg1: even divide, edge bit set.
CLKOUT0 Reg1: odd divide, no edge.
CLKOUT0 Reg1: odd divide, edge bit set.
DIV Reg2 HT/LT value for D=5.
Penalty for fractional M in score.

sectiondef.header: Math helper macros to avoid -lm dependency

briefdescription.para: Round double to nearest s32 integer.
Round double to nearest integer returned as double.
Floor of x returned as double.
Derive the K factor from the line rate.

detaileddescription.para: The K factor maps line rate ranges to a divider used to compute the target output frequency: Fout = LineRate / K.

parameterdescription.para: is the desired line rate in Mbps.

simplesect.para: K factor, or 0 if line rate is out of range.

briefdescription.para: Pack high-time and low-time values into a 16-bit register value.
Compute CLKOUT0 register values from the output divider.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to store the first register value.
is a pointer to store the second register value.

simplesect.para: OutputDivider is assumed to be integer only.

briefdescription.para: Compute the MMCM parameters and DRP writes for a given line rate.

parameterdescription.para: is the desired line rate in Mbps.

simplesect.para: MmcmResult structure with Valid set if a solution was found.
Uses double-precision floating point arithmetic matching the reference MMCM C-code algorithm.

briefdescription.para: This function configures the MMCM registers in the RX PHY for a given line rate.

detaileddescription.para: It computes the optimal MMCM parameters (M, D, O) and writes the corresponding values to the PHY MMCM registers.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Rx_Phy instance to operate on.
is the desired line rate in Mbps (400-4500 Mbps).

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if MMCM programming is successful.
XST_FAILURE if no valid MMCM solution is found.
